skill.name: azure-ai-vision-imageanalysis-java
skill.description: Build Java applications that use Azure AI Vision for captions, OCR, object detection, tags, people detection, and smart crops.
skill.summary: Use Azure AI Vision Image Analysis SDK examples in Java applications.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static command execution and weak cryptography alerts are false positives caused by Markdown code fences and ordinary SDK documentation text. The skill documents legitimate Azure AI Vision network calls and environment-based API key configuration, so it carries low operational risk but no confirmed malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Environment-Based API Key Configuration
low_findings.description: The examples read VISION_ENDPOINT and VISION_KEY from environment variables. This is normal Azure SDK practice, but users must protect the API key.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines clearly reference an Azure endpoint and API key. The usage is legitimate documentation, so the risk is limited to credential handling hygiene.

low_findings.title: Expected Outbound Azure Vision Requests
low_findings.description: The examples analyze image URLs and reference Azure service endpoints. Network use matches the stated purpose and does not show exfiltration or hidden destinations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Network behavior is explicit and tied to Azure AI Vision examples. I found no evidence of unrelated endpoints or secret forwarding.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives Dismissed
low_findings.description: The reported command execution and weak cryptography patterns occur in Markdown fences, feature names, and documentation text. No executable Ruby backticks or weak crypto calls are present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are visible Markdown or descriptive content, not runtime code inside the skill. There is no prompt injection or obfuscated execution path in SKILL.md.

content.user_title: Build Azure AI Vision Java Apps
content.value_statement: Java developers need fast examples for Azure AI Vision image analysis. This skill provides focused SDK patterns for captions, OCR, tags, objects, people, and smart crops.
actual_capabilities: Shows Maven dependency setup for the Azure AI Vision Image Analysis Java package.
Demonstrates client creation with API keys and DefaultAzureCredential.
Provides Java examples for captions, dense captions, OCR, tags, objects, people, and smart crops.
Shows synchronous and asynchronous image analysis request patterns.
Includes basic Azure SDK error handling with HttpResponseException.
limitations: It is documentation only and does not include a runnable sample project.
It requires an existing Azure AI Vision resource and valid credentials.
It does not cover deployment, billing, monitoring, or production architecture.
Some features depend on Azure regional availability and image requirements.

use_cases.title: Add Image Captions
use_cases.description: Create Java code that sends an image to Azure AI Vision and reads a caption response.

use_cases.title: Extract Text From Images
use_cases.description: Use OCR examples to read text lines, words, confidence values, and bounding polygons.

use_cases.title: Prototype Visual Metadata Pipelines
use_cases.description: Combine tags, objects, people detection, and smart crops for image indexing workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Client
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to create an Azure AI Vision ImageAnalysisClient in Java using environment variables.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze One Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Write Java code that analyzes a local image and returns a gender-neutral caption with confidence.

prompt_templates.title: Add OCR Handling
prompt_templates.prompt: Create Java code that extracts OCR text from an image and prints lines, words, confidence, and bounding polygons.

prompt_templates.title: Combine Visual Features
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a Java image analysis method that requests captions, tags, objects, and OCR, then handles Azure SDK errors.

output_examples.input: A request to caption a product image from a local file.
output_examples.output: A Java-focused answer that creates the client, loads the image, requests captions, and prints the caption confidence.

output_examples.input: A request to process receipts with OCR.
output: Client setup using Azure credentials from the environment.
OCR request flow for an image file.
Readable handling for text lines, words, confidence scores, and bounding polygons.

output_examples.input: A request to build an image metadata workflow.
output_examples.output: A concise implementation plan using tags, object detection, OCR, and captions with Azure service limitations noted.

best_practices: Use environment variables or managed identity instead of hardcoding Azure credentials.
Validate image format, dimensions, and file size before calling the service.
Check regional feature availability before deploying caption and dense caption features.
anti_patterns: Do not paste Azure API keys into source files or prompts.
Do not assume every Azure region supports every visual feature.
Do not send private images to cloud analysis without user consent and policy review.

faq.question: What Azure package does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It covers the com.azure azure-ai-vision-imageanalysis Java package.

faq.question: Does it support OCR examples?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes examples for the READ visual feature and OCR result traversal.

faq.question: Can it use managed identity?
faq.answer: Yes. It shows DefaultAzureCredential as an alternative to API key credentials.

faq.question: Does it include asynchronous examples?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes an async client example using analyzeFromUrl and subscription handlers.

faq.question: Does this skill execute Azure calls by itself?
faq.answer: No. It provides documentation examples that users can adapt in their Java projects.

faq.question: What should I check before production use?
faq.answer: Check image limits, regional feature support, credential storage, privacy needs, and error handling.
